summary: TourBuddy - a tourist safety platform. Building a React web dashboard for police/tourism authorities with incident tracking, geo-fencing alerts, risk heatmaps, and volunteer management on the Internet Computer using Motoko backend and Internet Identity authentication. Currently focusing on the front dashboard interface.
architectural_notes: Built on Internet Computer (ICP) using Motoko for backend logic
React frontend with Internet Identity authentication
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- TourBuddy shield-and-compass logo mark with a dark navy and amber color scheme, flat vector style (tourbuddy-logo.dim_256x256.png)

existing_features.title: Create a top navigation bar with the TourBuddy logo/brand name, a global alert status indicator (e.g., 'All Clear', 'Active Incidents'), and a user profile/logout area.
Create a left sidebar with navigation links for: Overview (home), Incidents, Geo-fence Alerts, Risk Heatmap, and Volunteer Management. Highlight the active section.

Create a main Overview page as the dashboard home. Display four KPI summary cards: Total Active Incidents, Active Geo-fence Alerts, Volunteers On Duty, and Tourist Areas Monitored. Each card shows a count and a trend indicator.

Create a Recent Incidents table section on the Overview page showing columns: Incident ID, Location, Type, Severity (color-coded: Low/Medium/High/Critical), Status, and Reported Time. Populate with realistic mock data.

Create a Risk Heatmap placeholder section that displays a stylized SVG-based zone map with color-coded risk zones (green = safe, yellow = caution, red = high risk) representing tourist areas. Use static mock data for zone risk levels.
Create an Active Geo-fence Alerts panel on the Overview page listing current alerts with zone name, alert type, triggered time, and a dismiss/acknowledge button (UI only, no backend).

Create a Volunteer Management section accessible from the sidebar, showing a list/table of volunteers with: Name, Badge ID, Area Assigned, Status (Active/Off Duty/On Break), and Last Check-in time. Populate with mock data.

Apply a cohesive visual theme across the entire dashboard: dark navy/slate background with amber/gold accents for alerts and highlights, clean sans-serif typography, card-based layout with subtle shadows and borders. The aesthetic should evoke authority and situational awareness appropriate for a safety command center.
Render the generated dashboard hero/logo image as a static asset from frontend/public/assets/generated.

new_feature_requests.summary: Front dashboard interface for police/tourism authorities
